description: Architectural and scope decisions, seeded from chat threads. Surfaced in the /dev mgmt panel. Mirror to the `decisions` SQLite table when needed for queryability.

decisions.decision: Use this Claude Code chat as the persistent project management thread; mirror decisions to dashboard.
decisions.rationale: Solo dev wants a single chronological record of why architecture is the way it is. Chat threads alone are ephemeral; pulling into repo + dashboard makes them durable + visible.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Free-form notes in a doc; Notion/Linear; nothing.

decisions.decision: Add management panel inside existing /dev view rather than creating a new /manage route.
decisions.rationale: /dev is already the builder-facing surface. Adding mgmt there keeps related concerns colocated. Promote to /manage later only if it grows enough to justify.
decisions.alternatives_considered: New /manage route; chat-only with no dashboard presence.

decisions.rationale: Same provider as tactical-executor — simplest cross-service env wiring, lowest friction.

decisions.decision: Stay on N8N → Gemini for production LLM calls.
decisions.rationale: Currently working and free-tier. Add retry/backoff and caching but don't change the path.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Migrate to direct Gemini API; multi-backend ensemble.

decisions.decision: Hybrid: flat feature vector under, 100-pt weighted display per framework §10.
decisions.rationale: Internal flat 0-1 vector preserves all signals for future fine-tuning. Display layer matches authored weights. As outcome data accrues, weights become learned per regime — path to a real model.

decisions.decision: Tier 1 (multi-agent in one process) now; design for Tier 3 (trained classifiers + fine-tuned LLMs) later.
decisions.rationale: Realistic v1 from cost/time. Heuristics where rules are clear (framework §5/§6/§12); LLM where judgment required. Trained models slot in as outcome data accrues.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Single LLM call; jump straight to Tier 3 with cold-start; microservices per agent.

decisions.decision: Two-repo split: this repo (input + dashboard) + new macro-brain repo (agents + orchestrator + models).
decisions.rationale: Different release cadences. Ingestion stable; brain models change weekly. Architecture doc already plans this.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Keep everything in one repo; repo-per-agent (microservices day one).

decisions.decision: Thesis Agent, Framework Agent, Application Agent (BE+FE combined). Documented in docs/agent_roster.md.
decisions.rationale: Each owns a distinct artifact type with focused scope, memory, tool allowlist. Cross-agent work requires explicit handoff.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Production-only agent split (no dev agents); separate Backend + Frontend agents.

decisions.decision: Manual execution throughout phases 1-7. Wire Trading-Agent-V1-CODEX in Phase 9 (last), only when criteria met.
decisions.rationale: Don't yet trust the brain's calls in live conditions. Manual = forced human review = catches systematic errors fast. Avoids regulatory/risk exposure of automated execution before earned.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Wire executor immediately; paper-trade in parallel from day one.

decisions.decision: Every LLM call must satisfy the logging contract (docs/logging_contract.md). agent_call_log table populated from first call. training_corpus/ accumulates for future fine-tuning.
decisions.rationale: Year-2 pivot from agents to fine-tuned LLMs becomes a short pivot, not a 6-month archeology project.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Defer logging discipline until later; ship fast, refactor logging when needed.

decisions.decision: Defer custom-trained classifiers (regime, hawkish/dovish, source attribution) until outcome data exists (Phase 8).
decisions.rationale: Avoid building in the dark. Need labeled data first. Use LLM-prompted baselines until then.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Start regime classifier now (5-10y FRED labels available); start all three classifiers immediately.

decisions.decision: Four workstreams: ① Inputs, ② Brain, ③ Dashboard, ④ Execution. Each with own deliverables.
decisions.rationale: Brain is one workstream not the whole project. Inputs and dashboard each need first-class attention. Execution explicitly scoped manual-first.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Single 'product' workstream with brain as centerpiece.

decisions.decision: Merge to main + push directly. Skip PR ceremony for solo work.
decisions.rationale: Solo dev. No team review. PR diff viewer doesn't help more than reading committed files. GitHub commit permalink works for phone review.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Push branch + open PR; never push, work locally only.

decisions.decision: Always use thommi2phones as the active gh account (the personal account that owns this repo).
decisions.rationale: highergroundbs is the work account; this is a personal project. Switching globally to thommi2phones avoids per-push auth wrangling.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Per-repo credential.username override; switch on demand.
